images.caption:
Figure 1:The Intra-Query Attention Dilution.Left:The sequential cognitive overload setup, where
models are prompted to solve multiple independent AIME 2024 problems within a single prompt.Right:Mathematical accuracy strictly on thefinalproblem of the sequence. While frontier APIs
like Gemini 3.1 Pro and GPT-5.4 exhibit strong resilience, Claude Opus 4.6 and all tested open-weights models suffer a degradation in reasoning
accuracy. This reasoning degradation seems to be scale-invariant across open weight models ranging from 7B to 120B parameters.
This suggests that prior reasoning steps permanently degrade subsequent reasoning ability, highlighting a
fundamental architectural need for explicit working memory isolation inside of model’s own chain-of-thought.
Figure 3:Full bars represent Achieved Accuracy on the AIME 2024 benchmark modified with our perturbations. Semi-transparent segments indicate model refusals or failed answers.
Figure 4:Vulnerability Profiling: Average accuracy on transformations from each of the 4 categories by model.
Figure 5:Average Accuracy Drop across all models and transformations.
Figure 5:Average Accuracy Drop across all models and transformations.

Figure 6:Mathematical accuracy on last problem in sequential overload. Number of problems asked is shown
as a sequence on the x-axis. Total accuracy drop from baseline is shown in red.

Figure 7:Reasoning Efficiency: Average output token length by task. On top of each bar is output prompt length in thousands of tokens. Values in the middle of each bar are the accuracy of the model on that transformation.
Figure 8:Agent Context Leaks (1/3): Example of thetask_boundarymechanism observed in Antigravity.
Figure 9:Agent Context Leaks (2/3): Observed internal reasoning steps. Images are displayed at full width for maximum readability of the internal thought traces.
Figure 10:Agent Context Leaks (3/3): Note "Wait, the system message explicitly said You have called 5 tools in a row without calling a task_boundary tool."
